entry.title: Unlocking the Future: Why Upskilling with SAP Build Apps is Essential
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, the demand for innovative and efficient business applications is higher than ever. With the ever-increasing need to streamline operations and enhance productivity, mastering modern app-building tools has become a critical skill for IT professionals and business leaders alike.

Why SAP Build Apps?
SAP Build Apps provide a robust, no-code platform that empowers developers and non-developers to create custom applications with ease. With intuitive drag-and-drop functionality, SAP Build Apps eliminates many of the complexities traditionally associated with app development, making it accessible for users of varying skill levels. By investing in SAP Build Apps, you're not just learning another tool—you're adopting a transformative skill that aligns with the future of business technology.

Learning Pathways to Success
To help you navigate your upskilling journey, the SAP Learning site with over 2 million learners offers free, self-paced learning journeys. These resources are designed to guide you from foundational knowledge to advanced competencies, ensuring you're fully equipped to build high-impact applications. Here are some must-explore Learning Journeys:

  • Compose and Automate with SAP Build: The No-Code Way: Perfect for those new to no-code app building, this journey introduces the core concepts and tools in SAP Build to help you quickly create and automate applications without complex coding.

  • Develop Apps with SAP Build Apps Using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity: This journey focuses on SAP Build’s drag-and-drop interface, teaching you how to design and deploy applications in a matter of hours. It's ideal for IT professionals looking to develop dynamic apps without diving deep into code.

  • Extending SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Apps and Key User Extensibility: For those working with SAP S/4HANA, this path delves into extending existing SAP applications, enabling you to build custom solutions that seamlessly integrate with SAP’s ecosystem.

SAP Certification: Validating Your Skills
For backend developers seeking to bolster their credentials, the SAP Certified Associate - Backend Developer for SAP Cloud Programming Model certification provides an excellent opportunity to validate your expertise. This certification covers essential skills in the SAP Cloud environment, offering recognition and career advancement for those committed to mastering the backend of SAP technologies.

The Benefits of Investing in SAP Build Apps Skills
Investing your time and effort into learning SAP Build Apps is a strategic move that promises substantial returns. By mastering this powerful tool, you'll be well-equipped to meet the modern demands of app development, drive business success, and propel your career to new heights. Not only does SAP Build Apps provide you with a sought-after skill set, but it also enables you to build adaptable solutions that keep your business agile and future-ready.

Ready to unlock your potential? Visit the SAP Build Apps page on the SAP Learning site and start your journey towards success today.

entry.title: Access Control option for workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
entry.content:
As organisations grow and diversify, so does the need for flexible, secure, and tailored access control within collaboration platforms.

SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced Edition addresses this need with its advanced workspace privacy and access options, tailored to meet a different range of business requirements. This post dives into how these features support unique access configurations, offering business leaders, employees, and external end users the control they need over collaborative workspaces.

Public vs. Private Workspaces: The Core Options

At the foundation of SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition are two main workspace types: Public and Private.

  • Public Workspaces: Ideal for general information accessible by all employees, such as HR policies, product updates, or company news. These spaces are searchable across the SAP Build Work Zone site, and ensure employees can easily locate essential resources.
  • Private Workspaces: Designed for limited access, these workspaces are only available to a selected group of members. Employees must be invited or added via a user list, with non-members unable to search or view the content, which helps ensure sensitive information stays within specified groups (e.g., finance teams, regional HR policies).

Worth mentioning, a user can also add private content in a public space, if he/she wants to share it with a smaller audience. This is done by selecting New Private Folder from the create content dropdown (as shown in the following screenshot)

 

External User Access

 In certain cases, companies may want to grant external users - such as suppliers, consultants, or partners - access to specific workspaces without exposing the entire SAP Build Work Zone site. By selecting an external access option (available for both Public and Private workspaces), workspace admins can securely share essential information or collaborate with users outside the organisation.

Workspaces with external access are marked as “External” to alert members to potential privacy considerations, helping them avoid sharing confidential information (see screenshot below).

Furthermore, as a workspace creator, you can also control whether other members can join the workspace and whether they can invite others without requiring your approval as the workspace admin. (See the screenshot below).

Customisable Collaboration Levels and Task Policy Management

Beyond access settings, SAP Build Work Zone provides collaboration level options tailored to the workspace's purpose and members’ needs:

  • Read-Only: Members can view content without contributing.-
  • Limited Collaboration: Members can interact with limited editing permissions.
  • Full Collaboration: Offers the highest level of engagement, allowing members to actively participate in workspace activities.

For workspaces with Limited or Full collaboration, Task Policy editing options further define control over task updates. Workspace administrators can decide if tasks should be editable by all members, limited to assignees and admins, or strictly restricted to creators and admins. This setup helps creating transparency and define task accountability according to the Workspace’s requirements (see screenshot below).

Upload and Approval Policies

Content-sharing policies are critical for managing sensitive information. Workspace admins have options to enforce upload and approval policies, which determine whether content (like PDFs, videos, or posts) can be shared freely by members or if admin review is required (see screenshot). This ensures that only authorised information appears in the workspace, particularly in cases where compliance or quality control is essential.

Non-Member Participation and Access Policies

Non-member participation provide additional layers of security and flexibility:

Collaboration Level: Admins can allow non-members to view content or restrict all collaborative tools and member details. For cases requiring transparency across a department or broader audience, enabling non-member access can facilitate information sharing without compromising security.

Access Policies: Some workspaces may require restricted access for specific employee subgroups (e.g., HR or Sales), instead of granting access to the full organisation. These access policies allow granular control of visibility and participation, ensuring the workspace meets unique business requirements.

These flexible customisations are designed to meet diverse organisational needs, whether that’s ensuring company-wide access to HR updates or creating secure, department-specific workspaces for internal projects or partnerships with external collaborators.

By leveraging these options, your organisation can optimise SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition to match your unique operational needs, balancing open collaboration with tailored, role-based access, thus protecting sensitive information and at the same time promoting effective teamwork.

SUMMARY

Decision Table: 

What I want to achieve?Which configurations I need:
A Workspace visible to all employees. Each employee could join and invite other members.

Create a Public Workspace + select checkbox:

  • Allow all members to invite users,
  • Allow users to join this workspace
A workspace visible to all employees, to but as a workspace admin I want to control who has access and I want to be the only one inviting members

Create a Public Workspace + leave unselected all other checkboxes.

A workspace which is visible to all employees AND external users (prior invitation)Create a Public Workspace + select checkbox:
  • External users can access this workspace and be invited as members
A workspace visible only to members and accessible via invitation. As a workspace admin, I want to be the only only one who can invite users (users cannot request to join).

Create a Private Workspace + leave unselected all other checkboxes.

A workspace visible only to members and accessible via invitation. Members can invite other users and users are able to request to join the workspace.

Create a Private Workspace + select checkbox:

  • Allow all members to invite users,
  • Allow users to request to join this workspace
A workspace which is visible to only to member BUT ALSO to some external users (prior invitation)Create a Private Workspace + select checkbox:
  • External users can access this workspace and be invited as members
A Workspace (private or public) to share information, in which members can only view and download resources.After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Read Only.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take some actions on existing content such as view, comment, download. They can also handle tasks but not create ones.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Limited.

Remember to select task policy according to needs:

  • Tasks are editable by creators and workspace admins. Status can be updated by task assignee.
  • Tasks are editable by assignees, creators, and workspace admins. Status can be updated by all members.
  • Task details and status can be updated by all members.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take all actions on content such as view, comment, download, edit, rename and delete. They can also create and handle tasks.

All members can upload content.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Full + select checkbox Upload Policy.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take all actions on content such as view, comment, download, edit, rename and delete. They can also create and handle tasks.

All members can upload content, but as an admin you want to approve content sharing.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Full + select checkbox Upload Policy and select checkbox Approval Policy.

In a public or Private space, you want to share some content visible only to a subset of specific users.

Click on the hamburger menu on your Workpage, select Content from the available options, click on the Create dropdown menu and select New Private Folder.

entry.title: The Path Forward: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA Are Retired: Here’s Your Next Step with SAP Build Process Automation 

As part of SAP's continuous innovation, SAP Build Process Automation has been introduced as the successor to SAP Workflow Management within the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). With the recent announcement of the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, now is the time for customers to plan their transition to SAP Build Process Automation. This next-generation tool is designed to bring powerful, unified capabilities for process automation, enabling you to build workflows, deploy automations, and enhance productivity across your organization.

Why Transition to SAP Build Process Automation?

With the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, SAP Build Process Automation has emerged as the comprehensive tool for creating intelligent workflows and automations. Designed to bring together workflow, RPA, and AI-driven process automation, SAP Build Process Automation enables business and IT teams to work collaboratively to simplify complex, repetitive tasks and drive efficiency like never before. 

If you're currently using SAP Workflow Management or Intelligent RPA, now is the time to explore how SAP Build Process Automation can empower your teams with its advanced capabilities. This transition also ensures ongoing support, access to the latest features, and alignment with SAP’s innovation roadmap. 

Join Our Exclusive Migration Webinars 

To help you make this transition seamless, we’re hosting a series of exclusive webinars. Our product management team will provide guidance on migrating to SAP Build Process Automation and answer any questions. You’ll gain insights into: 

  • Migration Strategies: How to move from SAP Workflow Management and Intelligent RPA to SAP Build Process Automation smoothly. 
  • Advanced Features: Explore new capabilities for workflow, RPA, and AI within the SAP Build environment. 
  • Best Practices: Get practical tips and resources to accelerate your migration journey. 

Mark your calendar and choose the session that fits your needs: 

SAP Workflow Management Migration Webinar                             SAP Intelligent RPA Migration Webinar 

📅Date: Tuesday, December 3, 2024                                                     📅  Date: Thursday, December 5, 2024 

⏰Time: 10 am EST                                                                                ⏰Time: 10 am EST   

[Register Here]                                                                                         [Register Here] 

 

Additional Resources 

To further support your migration journey, we’ve curated helpful resources: 

  • SAP Workflow Management - The Path forward
  • Transition to SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Blog post on SAP Community  

Don’t miss this opportunity to learn how SAP Build Process Automation is transforming the automation landscape. Whether you’re in business or IT, these webinars are designed to give you the insights and support you need to make a successful transition. 

Let’s make automation simpler, smarter, and more powerful with SAP Build Process Automation. Register today to unlock the full potential of SAP Build for your organization!

entry.title: Integration of ABAP world in SAP Build
entry.content:
As you might have heard the big announcement from Michael Ameling in this year's SAP TechEd, we are now bringing ABAP world and SAP Build together. If you missed, please check out his blog, where he has provided a little bit of history and the context of integration.

This Blog would cover how these two worlds SAP Build and ABAP are coming together. We will show how if you are a Build developer or ABAP developer or if you want to extend your ERP solutions with ABAP you would be able to so now starting from the Build Lobby.

Why do we want to integrate?

Before we get into creating a package from SAP Build, it would be great to know the reasons for this and the improvements it would bring to the developer community.

1> SAP Build is to become the Entry point for all Developers whether you are a professional developer who loves to code or you are a business user or citizen developer who would like to build applications with minimum to no coding.


SAP BuildSAP Build Portfolio

 


With SAP Build Process Automation we provide a way to automate business processes and  workflows etc. With SAP Build Apps we help you developer wonderful mobile and web apps. With SAP Build Code we enable professional developers to create wonderful applications using Java and JavaScript. 
Now with ABAP also integrated, professional developers are now able to create ABAP packages directly from the SAP Build lobby and start their ABAP development from there.

2> Fusion Development - We know that development work cannot be done in silos. We need to have all the capabilities offered, so that developers could decide how best to achieve a business outcome. With this thought, here in SAP Build when you are working on ABAP, you will not be just developing ABAP packages, but also be actively using it across all SAP Build in a seamless integration.

Fusion Development

 

 

With BAS Service Center, you would be able to integrate ABAP services. When developing UI applications for RAP services, you can now directly open SAP Build Code from the service binding preserving the context of the service and system. This improves the integration of the backend RAP services with frontend Fiori Applications.
You could also wrap the ABAP RAP service bindings as an Action and integrate it in SAP Build Process Automation or SAP Build Apps to automate or build mobile apps with a ABAP Cloud service built in SAP Build.

This ABAP integration is not just for BTP ABAP Cloud, but even S/4 HANA Cloud systems could be accessed and worked upon from SAP Build. 

3> ERP Extensibility - SAP Build is the solution for all S/4HANA Cloud extensibility solutions. Through SAP BUILD Extensibility Wizard, users can now  extend Custom Process, Custom Field and Custom Logic covering both on stack and side by side extensibility. With ABAP integration, this extensibility feature becomes even more powerful. 
Users can now build and extend ABAP services directly from S/4 HANA applications via SAP Build and integrate it to develop their custom extensions.

SAP Extensibility with ABAP

 

Now you know the reasons, let's now get into development. But before that make sure you complete the following pre requisites:

Prerequisites: 

  1. You've subscribed to one of the services in SAP Build. For more information, see What Is SAP Build?.
  2. You have downloaded one of the latest milestone from here and install ADT from the customer update site (https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest). Make sure you have selected Eclispe+Command in Link Handlers settings for ADT.
  3. You've created a destination in the SAP BTP cockpit. Please follow the following steps for BTP ABAP and S/4 HANA public Cloud:
    1. Destination for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 
      1. Entitle SAP BTP ABAP Environment to your Global Subaccount; https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/getting-started-with-customer-account-in-abap-environment?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 
      2. Create a Service Instance: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/creating-abap-system?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 
      3. Setup Destination in BTP to connect from BTP to ABAP System

        1. Destination Properties
        • Update additional properties:

         


        It should look like:

    2. Destination for S/4 HANA Public Cloud
      1. Prerequisite: You should have a connection to S/4 HANA Public Cloud system. 
      2. Create a Destination as above with same properties. Only changes are:
        In the url section suffix -api after the system host For ex: my3xxxxx-api.s4hana.ondemand.com
        In audience section add the exact system url without -web or -api.

  4.  Establish a communication system to establish connectivity from ABAP system to BTP
    1. In the SAP BTP cockpit, download your subaccount-specific trust certificate by navigating to Connectivity --> Destinations -->  Download Trust.
    2. In the SAP Fiori launchpad of your ABAP environment system, create a communication system by using the Communication Systems app. See How to Create Communication Systems.
    3. Update Technical Data
      1. For  BTP ABAP Environment  
        1. Navigate to Destination Service
          1. Check “Use Default Instance”.
          2. Update Name by selecting the name of destination configured in Step 3 configured above.

        2. Navigate to Identity Provider
          1. Choose SAML Assertion Provider and update the details:
            1. User Id Mapping Mode: UserName
            2. SAML Bearer Issuer: Upload the Destination trust certificate downloaded above, copy the CN value and paste in the input field.
          2. Save the above changes.
            It would look like this:
        3. Communication System
      2. For S/4HANA Public Cloud 
        1. Navigate to General
          1. Check for Inbound only
        2. Navigate to Identity Provider
          1. Choose SAML Assertion Provider and update the details:
            1. User Id Mapping Mode: UserName
            2. SAML Bearer Issuer: Upload the Destination trust certificate downloaded above, copy the CN value and paste in the input field.
          2. Save the above changes


  5.  You have connected to ABAP System from ADT :
    1. BTP ABAP Cloud  
    2. SAP S4HANA CLOUD 

 

Build your first ABAP service in Build 

ABAP project creation :

In BTP, for any of Build Subscription, click on "Go to Application", it will open a Build Lobby page 

Click on Create Button, it will open a Creation Wizard, select "Build an Application" and then "ABAP Cloud" from the given options:

On Selection of ABAP Cloud:  Project Creation wizard would appear:

The System Dropdown would show the list of Destinations which you have connected to BTP ABAP Environment or S/4 HANA Public Cloud. Make a selection of the Destination which is pointing to your desired ABAP System.

If the destination is valid you will see the System Id and System Client below the System's Dropdown

For a valid System you have two options:

a> You can use a preexisting package which you have already created via ADT 

On Clicking on next you would be directly moved to Build ABAP project creation dialog, details below

b> You can create a new package for a given superpackage:

On Clicking  of next, you will be asked to either enter a preexisting Transport Request  or enable creation of new Transport Request:

If you know the Transport Request, you could select it from the drop down. 
If not, then you could provide the description, a new Transport Request would be generated at the time of package creation.

After clicking on next, Build ABAP Project Creation Dialog would appear. 

This  is Build ABAP Project, This will map and point to the ABAP package which you are creating for a given system. This way you will have a Build ABAP project always referencing to an ABAP package.

In a new tab you will get a prompt to open ADT:

On Selecting "Open Eclipse" another prompt would come to run Eclipse Command , please select OK.
It wil then go to your installed ADT and asked to select an ABAP project 

It will then Open ABAP Repository Object Generator Wizard for ODATA Ui Service 

If you have a reference object, you can select and press Next and proceed or if you want start fresh , you could Cancel.  
You will find the package is created in ADT for your system 

You can now work on the ADT package as you would have done earlier.


ABAP Project navigation

In Lobby page you will also have a Build ABAP Cloud project with the name provided above already created :

 If you click on the ABAP project which you have created it will navigate and open the same referenced ABAP package in ADT 


ABAP Project reference object mapping 

 You can also map existing SAP BUILD ABAP Cloud project to an existing Service Binding object for future reference . 
Click on the Build ABAP project , it would open a Project Details pane . You can click on the Reference Object Name dropdown, if you have an existing Service Bindings for the the ABAP package , you will see them in the list.
Select one.



After the property is updated, it will now update the navigation link. It will now point to the Service Binding which you have selected. Clicking on the project would open the Service Binding Object in ADT:

 

Conclusion 

Now you have build your first ABAP package via build. You can now build any service bindings. As part of fusion development these services could be used across all Build products. If you have a need for extension using these Service , you can use it too. 
More on these in subsequent blogs.

Stay tuned for many exciting developments in this area. We are just getting started.

entry.title: Enabling Access to On-Premise S/4HANA Applications in SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
entry.content:
Subscriptions

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Cloud Connector
  • SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
  • S/4 HANA On-Premises System Setup

Pre-requisite Setup

  • Setup of SAP Business Technology Platform Account
  • Setup of SAP Cloud Connector & Trust to the SAP S/4HANA System
  • Setup of S/4HANA on-premises System
  • Setup SAP Identity and Authentication Service (optional)

Pre-requisite Checks

  1. In your BTP subaccount you are subscribed to the following subscriptions
  • Cloud Identity Services

  • SAP Business Application Studio (Automatically Added)
  • SAP Build Work Zone, Standard edition

SAP BTP Subaccount --> Instances and Subscriptions --> create

 

  • Environment – SAP Cloud Foundry Instance

  1. Configure Cloud Connector with S/4 HANA On-Premise System with the following details –

For the installation of the Cloud Connector please follow the steps in the SAP Help document SAP Help

You can skip installing the Cloud Connector if you’re using a demo backend system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL). These demo systems come with a pre-installed Cloud Connector that’s ready to use. To access it, go to the Welcome page in your CAL system, where you’ll find the relevant links. The Cloud Connector typically runs at http://<IP of your SAP CAL backend>:8443/, where you can directly connect to it.

 

  1. Check your BTP Connectivity with Cloud Connector:- login to your BTP Subaccount and go to Destinations, and select  Create Destination with the following details :

 

 

Field

Value

Name

Name of the Destination

Type

HTTP

URL

http://Virtual Host Port No.(443XX)

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

Support User Created in On-Premise System

 

 

           

 

  1. Once the Connection is successful, test the mapping created in Cloud Connector, it should be reachable.

 

 

Execution Steps

 

Create a Site in SAP Build Work Zone

In SAP Build Work Zone, a "site" refers to a digital workspace or portal where users can access applications, content, and resources. It is the central hub for delivering a personalized and unified experience to employees, partners, or customers. Sites in SAP Build Work Zone are highly customizable and can be designed to meet specific user needs and business requirements.

  1. Login to SAP BTP Subaccount and go to instances and subscriptions, choose SAP Build Work Zone and Go to Application

In a new window, you will log in to SAP Build Work Zone Site.

Here Select Create Site.

Keep the default settings for the site same and your site is created.

Note – Make sure you have the necessary authorizations assigned to your user ID in SAP BTP. The following roles are mandatory – Launchpad_Admin, Launchpad_External_User

In SAP BTP Subaccount to Security --> Users --> select Assign User Collection

 

Assign these roles to your User ID.

Create Destinations in SAP BTP

To display different types of app interfaces in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (like on-premise SAP S/4HANA apps and cloud-based apps), you first need to set up a way for Work Zone to access them. This is done by creating HTTP connections, called as "runtime destination, design time destination" in the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) cockpit. This connection acts as a bridge, allowing Work Zone to securely access and display apps from both your on-premise systems and the cloud.

Creating Design-time Destination

The design-time destination defines the location from which to fetch the content that is exposed by the content provider in SAP Build Work Zone.

  1. In the SAP BTP cockpit, log on to your subaccount as administrator.
  2. Go to Connectivity --> Destinations.
  3. To configure the design-time destination, choose New Destination and enter the following information:

 

Field

Value

Name

Add the dt suffix to the name

Type

HTTP

URL

https://<VIRTUAL_HOST>:<VIRTUAL_PORT>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

To connect to the SAP S/4HANA system, use a technical user on the destination with permission to read the CDM, for example, the technical user with the SAP_FLP_EXP_USER role, such as <SAP S/4HANA user ID> Creating System User in SAP

 

 

By choosing Additional Properties  New Property -->  sap-client: <client number>

Check the Connection for the Destination.

 

Creating Run-time Destination

The runtime destination defines the location from which to consume the content required to run the federated apps in runtime.

  1. To configure the runtime destination, choose New Destination and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Name

Add the rt suffix to the name

Type

HTTP

URL

https://<VIRTUAL_HOST>:<VIRTUAL_PORT>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

To connect to the SAP S/4HANA system, use a technical user on the destination with permission to read the CDM, for example, the technical user with the SAP_FLP_EXP_USER role, such as <SAP S/4HANA user ID> Creating System User in SAP

 

Add Following Additional Properties to complete configuration of runtime destination.

Field

Value

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

sap-client

Client No

sap-platform

ABAP

sap-sysid

On-Premise System ID

sap-service

32XX (XX- virtual Port)

And Check the Connection for this destination it should be successful.

 

Note - The destination name must contain only lowercase letters and numbers. Do not use the underscore (_) character.

Expose Authorization from On-Premise System

This step makes your on-premise SAP roles accessible in SAP Build Work Zone, allowing end users to access integrated Fiori applications directly within the Work Zone site.

  1. Login to SAP On-Premise system and go to T-code - /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE

Add the relevant SAP Roles you want to expose to SAP Build Work Zone.

  1. Click on Expose Content

Make sure the execution is without any errors.

Configure Site in Work Zone

  1. Log in to your SAP Build Work Zone Instance, navigate to Content Channel --> New --> Content Provider

 

  1. Add the Following Details and Save.

Note – Please specify your correct runtime and design time destinations created for build work zone.

  1.  Update the content, here the content provider should get updated without any errors.

In case you receive any errors in connection please verify your destination names , for mapping errors check connectivity with the cloud connector.

Once the content is updated you will have your roles exposed from on premise available in work zone site.

  1. Update the Content From Content Provider

Select your content provider

Add your exposed roles

Roles are now visible in content manager

  1. Go to site Directory --> Settings --> Edit

The exposed roles will be available,

Assign them to the site and Save.

  1. Add the roles in your BTP subaccount

Login to your BTP Subaccount with administrator user and go to security --> Users --> Assign Role Collections

Assign the exposed SAP roles to the user

  1. Now in your SAP Build Work Zone Site the apps are visible

Thank You.

entry.title: 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users
entry.content:
2024 was an action-packed year in SAP Business Technology Platform solutions, with great technology updates to help you think ahead, adapt and keep things running smoothly.  This article highlights some of the new features released in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) solutions between January 2024 and November 2024 that are most relevant for SAP S/4HANA users.

As a quick recap, SAP Business Technology Platform brings together application development, automation, integration, data and analytics, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for SAP applications. SAP BTP is designed to complement both SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition.  There are more than 80 different services in SAP BTP, in this article I'll focus on SAP Integration Suite, SAP Business AI, SAP Build, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP DataSphere, and SAP HANA Cloud.

So, what’s new? Let’s explore them now!

SAP Integration Suite: Supporting a wider range of integration needs

As a critical part of SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite has been a reliable source of integration technology for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. And the introduction of new features is making these connections more streamlined, situation aware, and productive—no matter how complex the landscape.

  • Event-driven architecture
    SAP Integration Suite now supports event-driven architecture to enable real-time processes that scale efficiently across distributed environments. It includes advanced event mesh capabilities that allow organizations to handle peak loads with greater ease and run more responsive and resilient operations.

  • Expanded integration library
    The curated library of integrations now includes ServiceNow and Workday solutions, expanding our portfolio to more than 3,000 connectors. Built and maintained by SAP and our partners, these integrations simplify connectivity to third-party applications—enabling smoother workflows and faster deployment.

  • On-premise solution workload management
    Extending beyond cloud-based connections, SAP Integration Suite supports ground-to-ground processes so companies can connect on-premise systems directly. This functionality provides greater flexibility for organizations with hybrid or fully on-premise environments.

  • API fabric for centralized access
    The API fabric enhances developer productivity by centralizing SAP and third-party APIs in a unified catalog. Developers can now quickly discover, register, and access a broad array of APIs, simplifying integration development and accelerating project timelines.

  • EOIO processing
    To support complex asynchronous integration scenarios, SAP Integration Suite now includes “exactly once in order” (EOIO) processing. This capability helps ensure messages are delivered exactly once and in the correct sequence while reducing data errors and enhancing consistency.

🔖Learn more about SAP Integration Suite's new features.

SAP Business AI: Making autonomous finance a reality

 The introduction of autonomous finance capabilities in SAP BTP is making a finance function that requires minimal human intervention a practical and productive reality. Enabled by the addition of SAP Business AI, this update automates complex financial operations—helping companies run with improved agility and resilience.

Leveraging the advanced AI and machine learning models of SAP Business AI, the platform provides SAP S/4HANA Cloud with tools that automate routine tasks, such as transaction processing and reconciliations. ​Recent benchmarking of SAP customers shows that adopting these capabilities paves the way to 90% more-efficient financial reporting, 7% higher forecast accuracy, and 10 times faster planning cycles.

In addition, the enhanced predictive capabilities and real-time data analytics available in SAP BTP equip SAP S/4HANA Cloud with insights that go beyond traditional finance automation. Organizations can now manage risk, increase transparency, and improve accuracy as the cloud ERP anticipates financial outcomes and adapts proactively to changes in cash flow or market volatility.

🔖Learn more about autonomous finance

SAP Build: Stepping up development with ABAP and generative AI

SAP Build solutions have become critical tools for the fast development of applications and automations with a simplified and streamlined builder interface.  Announced in October 2024, The latest integration of ABAP development tools and generative AI in SAP Build is making that experience even better for developers of all skill levels looking to modernize and extend their ERP capabilities.

By connecting tools such as ABAP, Java, JavaScript, and low-code in a unified environment, SAP Build enables direct development access within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making customization faster and easier. The extensibility wizard allows extensions for fields, logic, and processes while maintaining core system integrity.

Prebuilt content in SAP Build accelerates work across functions, including HR, spend management, and procurement. For example, the Joule copilot boosts efficiency with code completion, workflow automation, and instant help access. Developers can also create custom AI skills with drag-and-drop simplicity.

🔖Learn more the latest in SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA

SAP Datasphere: Making data handling simpler and more reliable

SAP Datasphere focused on improving access to mission-critical business data across any cloud infrastructure with a business data fabric.  In July 2024, the following updates gave business users stronger control over data views, resources, and import flexibility.

  • Compatibility contracts
    By setting a data view’s release state to “Released,” business users can help ensure output columns remain consistent until they’re replaced by a successor. This feature also enables gradual decommissioning while maintaining system integrity.

  • Workload management
    Resource management can be centralized by defining priorities and statement limits for all spaces. In return, business users can manage workload distribution effectively, while deprecating parameters for the command-line interface simplify updates in future releases.

  • Space changes during content import
    Specific spaces can now be chosen during imports. Space mapping added flexibility to support intra-tenant transport. Business users can import content into different spaces within the same tenant, even creating multiple copies in various spaces if needed.

🔖Learn more about SAP Datasphere's new features

SAP Analytics Cloud: Improving insight depth and speed

SAP Analytics Cloud, also part of SAP BTP, has been enhanced with several powerful updates that streamline data analysis, planning, and presentation. With these new features, SAP Analytics Cloud is adding even more depth and flexibility for customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making it easier for teams across the business to access insights and make data-driven decisions.

Here’s a look at some of the most impactful advancements:

  • Report-report interface
    Organizations with live connections to the SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) application can now leverage the report-report interface to analyze data in context. With this feature, users can jump from making queries in SAP BW—from generating ABAP reports and transactions to analyzing data.

  • Natural language queries
    Enabled by generative AI, the just ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud allows users to ask questions in their natural language and receive instant data-driven answers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud while reducing their dependence on IT support. Recent updates include tools to simplify the user experience, such as the prompt guide, model control options for administrators, and advanced comparisons.

  • Plan-data files uploading
    Starting with the Q3 2024 release, business users are now able to enhance their planning efficiency by uploading their plan-data files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly into SAP Analytics Cloud. They can initiate uploads through triggers in stories, and administrators can set up data structures and upload jobs to help ensure data consistency.

  • Standard unit conversion
    The standard unit conversion feature simplifies data handling by allowing modelers and planners to use predefined conversion tables for measurements like length and weight. This capability helps ensure consistent unit representation across reports and stories, supporting data accuracy in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud (part 2): Extending planning and analysis business-wide

This year’s updates also brought powerful enhancements for planning and analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud. The following innovations were added to SAP Analytics Cloud to further streamline workflows, foster collaboration, and support strategic insights across departments, particulary for users with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Triggering of calendar events from external applications
    Enhancing workflow automation for enterprise planning scenarios, this capability simplifies end-to-end planning processes and helps ensure key tasks are executed without disruption across integrated platforms.

  • Automated synchronization of calendar events
    With automatic synchronization of SAP calendar events with Microsoft Teams and Outlook, users can keep track of critical planning deadlines and events. This integration improves productivity and collaboration, embedding SAP planning directly into users’ daily calendars.

  • Industry-specific business content
    New and updated content packages in SAP Analytics Cloud provide prebuilt templates and analytics tools for business areas, such as sustainability, finance, sales, and the public sector. These packages accelerate the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, allowing companies to leverage industry-specific insights more quickly and effectively.

  • Probability simulation
    The compass functionality of SAP Analytics Cloud facilitates probability simulations that allow organizations to anticipate risks and model scenarios across key business drivers. Decision-makers can simulate the best, worst, and realistic outcomes—leading to more-proactive strategies to mitigate emerging disruptions.

  • Continuous planning integration
    The integration feature for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere forms a unified, governed data environment for planning and analytics. By centralizing data on one platform, this feature fosters collaboration across departments and reduces time-to-action for mission-critical decisions.

🔖Learn more about the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP HANA Cloud: Extending AI support and larger data volumes

 

SAP HANA Cloud is SAP’s database as a service solution, designed for large scale deployments.  The new features in SAP HANA Cloud for 2024 provide improved support for artificial intelligence and larger deployements, along with many quality of life features. Here are just a few of the highlights:

  • General availability of the Vector Engine for SAP HANA Cloud
    The introduction of the vector engine allows SAP HANA Cloud to provide the foundation for large language models, facilitating more contextually accurate responses to business queries. Learn more in this related blog post.

  • Support for larger instances
    With the 2024 Q2 release, the amount of memory available to allocate to a database instance increased to 12 TB. This allows better support for memory-intensive workloads.
  • Cloud Reliability – Backup Compression
    New backup compression for data, log, and catalog backups for SAP HANA database instances in SAP HANA Cloud reduces the amount of backup storage consumed, while ensuring the integrity and security of the backups.

  • Online migration
    The newly introduced online migration capabilities allow critical business processes to remain operational within the source SAP HANA system during the migration process. This innovation minimizes migration downtime, automates migration tasks, and reduces the need for manual interventions.

  • New machine learning options
    Multiple new features to the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) in SAP HANA Cloud facilitates faster modeling and application of AutoML, and automate analytics processes. The features allow a single neural network model to predict multiple related target columns. Other enhancements include the hierarchical density-based clustering of data with noise (HDBSCAN) and support for clustering of vector data using the DBSCAN and HDBSCAN functions.
    Learn more in the blog post provided by Christoph Morgen, as well as in this community post
  • Support for AWS PrivateLink services
    This new capability enables users to connect applications running within an Amazon Web Services (AWS) to SAP HANA Cloud using private IP endpoints, enhancing security. Learn more in this demo video.

Many more new features are described by @thomashammer in his blog post series.  

🔖 Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud

Closing

These new features in SAP BTP don't just make it easier to get the most from the data and processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud—they also build a solid foundation for growth and adaptability. When you use these tools together, you’re creating an ERP experience that’s ready for whatever comes next.

Learn more about how SAP BTP enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud at sap.com/btp-for-s4hana.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP

entry.title: How to scrape images from Google
entry.content:
Someone asked me how to scrape images from Google. I took it as a challenge.

Note that I did an image search, and took the Base64 versions of the images from the search page, which is how Google displays them. These are small, maybe 300-pixels square. If you wanted larger images, you'd extend the automation to click on the image to open the side panel, and use the right-click menu to save/download the image (my next challenge 😺). 

  1. Open Google.
  2. Enter your keyword in the search bar (the keyword comes from an input parameter).
  3. Click somewhere to get rid of the dropdown that hides the search button.
  4. Click "Google Search".
  5. Now on the results page, click "Images".
  6. Wait – before I put this 1s wait, my automation was quite unstable, sometimes worked, sometimes didn't. I believe not all the elements were in place at the beginning so waiting made sure they were loaded.
  7.  
  8. Iterate over all the pictures. I declared as an element anything with class = YQ4gaf – this was contained in the img tags.
  9. I retrieved the src property for the img tag – which was the image in Base64.
  10. I decoded the base64.
  11. I wrote the file to the path that was sent as an input parameter.
  12. Finally I set a condition so I would only loop over the first 10 images (well, 11 since I set the condition wrong 😮.

I then created a process with a trigger form with 2 inputs, the keyword for the Google search and the local path to store the pictures. But I could almost as easily create a Automation Launcher and User Task so I could trigger it attended whenever I wanted. 

 

How I Put It Together

Above looks like I knew what I was doing. But it took a little while, especially the following parts:

  • Recording the screens – this was BY FAR the most complicated part for me. I've tried the tutorials here and here, and hey are great, but I am still really not sure of myself on how the recording of screens works and the selecting of elements. Bu I'm getting there. I will work on it and create some videos on the opic.
  • Decoding Base64 – I was not sure how this was done, since we have documentation on using ctx library to do encoding/decoding, but I soon figured out how to use the Decode activity. I was already a little familiar with Base64 from SAP Build Apps, for which I created a blog on handling images, including in Base64 format.
  • Simulating Enter Key: This I did not even try. I would have needed it because when you enter a keyword the "Google Search" button is hidden, but online people said this was an issue. Instead, I simply clicked the Google logo to get rid of the dropdown that hid the button, and then used a click activity on the search button. 

 

Recording Screens

I did a recording by opening up Google in a new browser, and selecting Create → Application and selecting Recorder (not manual capture) and it created 2 screens – the Google home page, and then the results page.

On the results page, I created 2 captures. The main results page, and then the results page for images.

I also declared some elements I needed, like the search box and button. Most importantly, I declared the list of images, using the class as a criteria, and setting this to a collection.

 

Automation and Process

The automation is created for you, based on the recording, but I found there were all kinds of extra artifacts that I needed to delete or combine. Not a big deal, just something you have to do.

And then I had to add the part where we iterate through the images, decode the Base64 strings, save them to a file, and add a condition to just take the first 10 images. All using simple activities. I also created an input parameter for the keyword, instead of the hard-coded "cat" I used in the recording.

I created the artifacts for enabling this as an attended automation, creating:

  • Automation Launcher
  • User task (for entering the keyword and path)

The automation launcher you would have to register in the Control Tower for your environment.

And the user task you would have to add to your automation. Here is an example from my SAP CodeJam demo of sending emails using an automation.

entry.title: Site Menu in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
entry.content:
The Site Menu in SAP Build Work Zone is more than a navigation tool; it serves as the gateway for users to access a wide range of features, applications, and resources on the platform. Understanding how to configure, customize, and manage the Site Menu is essential for administrators aiming to create a seamless, role-specific, and intuitive user experience while enabling an efficient digital workspace.

In SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, administrators have various options to add, organize, and manage menu items, tailoring navigation to meet the unique needs of different user groups. This blog post provides an overview of these capabilities.

 

Overview

The Site Menu in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, serves as the primary navigation aid for users, providing them with quick access to workspaces, applications, and key resources. A well-organized Site Menu enhances user experience by making navigation intuitive and reducing the time it takes for users to find what they need. Users can access the menu, by the small edit icon on the right side of the top navigation bar.

 

How to configure site menu capabilities

Configuring the Site Menu involves a few essential steps:

Accessing the Site Menu Configuration: Company administrators can access the Site Menu configuration panel directly within the SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition interface. From here, they can review the current menu structure and make the necessary adjustments.

Editing Menu Items: Administrators have full control over the menu, allowing them to add, remove, disable, duplicate or reorder items. Each menu item can be linked to various types of content, including applications, workspaces, or external URLs. 

Setting Permissions: Administrators can configure access permissions for each menu item, ensuring that users only view content relevant to their specific roles and access levels.

The out-of-the-box "Home" section in the Site Menu is available by default but if a different landing page is preferred, the "Home" section can be disabled and replaced with other content, such as a custom workpages created in the site menu content.

 

 

Understanding the different Menu Item types

To create and effective and  user-friendly Site Menu, there are different types of menu a user can choose from, the most important being Custom, Workspace, Application, and Predefined. These items play significant roles in tailoring the navigation experience to meet the specific needs of different user groups and provide flexibility to the diverse business needs of our customers. Here's a detailed look at each of these menu item types:

Custom

Description: Custom menu items provide maximum flexibility, allowing administrators to link to a wide range of content both within and outside of the SAP Build Work Zone environment. These items can direct users to custom URLs or specific workpages stored within the site menu content.

Best use case: integrating unique resources like company intranet workpages, custom-built applications, or specialized informational workpages that don't fit into standard categories like applications or workspaces.

Workspaces

Description: Workspace menu items link directly to specific workspaces within SAP Build Work Zone. Workspaces are collaborative areas that contain various applications, information, and tools designed to support specific business processes, projects, or team activities.

Best use case: providing quick access to collaborative environments, helping users navigate to project areas, departmental resources, or group-specific workspaces. These type enhances collaboration and productivity by centralizing access to related resources.

Application

Description: Application menu items link directly to individual applications within SAP Build Work Zone, including standard SAP apps, custom-developed solutions, or third-party applications integrated into the platform.

Best use case: quick, easy access to tools needed for daily tasks. Application items simplify the UX by reducing the steps required to launch frequently used applications, improving overall efficiency.

Predefined Menu Items

Description: Predefined menu items are system-generated links that provide access to commonly used SAP Build Work Zone features or services. These items, like the Recommendation feature or the cross-workspace Knowledge Base, are automatically included in the Site Menu for consistent access to key functionalities.

Best use case: ensuring all users have standardized access to core features such as the Knowledge Base or workspace dropdowns. They maintain a consistent navigation structure, making it easier for users to find essential services across the platform.

 

Transporting Site Menu Configurations

Transporting Site Menu configurations is essential for maintaining consistency across different environments (for example, from a development to a production environment). This process involves:

Exporting the Configuration: Utilize the transport functionality within the Site Manager to export the current Site Menu configuration. This generates a file containing all menu settings and structures.

You have two options: either export all content items from the Menu Content screen or export only the content referenced by the menu. This choice is determined by checking or unchecking the corresponding checkbox in the prompted popup.

Importing to the Target System: In the destination system, use the Site Menu configuration screen to import the file. This applies the exported menu settings to the target system, ensuring a seamless  user experience across environments.

  

 

Best Practices for Site Menu Management:

  • Keep It Simple: Aim for a Site Menu structure that is easy to understand and navigate. Overcomplicating the menu can make it harder for users to find what they need.
  • Regularly Review and Update: As the needs of your organization change, so should your Site Menu structure and content to ensure it remains relevant and useful.
  • Engage Users for Feedback: Users feedback is invaluable to understand their real needs and preferences and optimize the Site Menu.

 

If you need additional information on workspaces, you can access the SAP Help Portal:

Overview of site menu

If you have more questions on SAP Build Work Zone, visit our FAQ page on SAP Community.

More Information on new Features and Functionalities:

Check out the roadmap for standard & advanced edition.

entry.title: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I have published 2 short videos on the integration of SAP BTP Document Management Services with SAP Build Process Automation.

The first video explains how to connect DMS to SAP Build Process Automation, so that the File widget is available to add to forms.

The second video explains how to add the File widget to a form, how you can add an uploaded document to another form, how the document is represented by a text string (this is important when connecting to automations), and how the document is stored in the DMS repo.

These videos are follow-ups to the 3 Tech Byte videos on setting up DMS, and explaining the 3 forms of DMS: Integration, Application and Repository options.

entry.title: How Fusion Development Boosts Developer Productivity
entry.content:
We’ve all been there: endless stakeholder requirements and not nearly enough developers to tackle them all. That’s why we continuously evolve the developer experience— to increase flexibility, improve the developer experience, and empower contribution and collaboration in a maximally productive development process. By providing intuitive tools, seamless integrations and experiences, we’re breaking down silos for a faster, more flexible development process tailored to individual skills and preferences. 

Recent announcements around SAP Build have elevated the developer experience to new heights, introducing features that significantly boost productivity in fusion teams. The SAP Build lobby, already a hub for creating, deploying, monitoring, and managing SAP Build projects, now serves as the starting point for ABAP Cloud projects, as well. Designed with fusion development in mind, which is a must-have for modern application development, the SAP Build lobby makes team collaboration on enterprise applications even more seamless. With the inclusion of ABAP environments, the possibilities for innovation have never been more exciting!  

 

What is Fusion Development? 

Fusion development can mean different things: On the one hand, it bridges different tools and technologies, on the other, it brings together people with diverse skills and preferences to collaborate effortlessly. All in all, it allows to leverage diverse expertise and technologies to build cohesive solutions.  

Picture a full-stack application with distinct frontend and backend components developed in separate environments. In this case, a developer or a fusion team could be using SAP Build Code and the ABAP environment – either on-stack (in SAP S/4HANA Cloud) or side-by-side (on SAP BTP). If adding low-code application development to the mix, the user interface could also be created in SAP Build Apps for drag-and-drop simplicity. Another example is an on-stack extension where a key user adds a custom field, which is then seamlessly exposed in a mobile application running on SAP BTP. 

The SAP Build lobby streamlines these processes by exposing extension assets for further editing or consumption. With ABAP Cloud now part of this unified development experience, SAP’s fusion development approach has become even more compelling.  

Figure 1: Examples of Fusion Development 

 

Why Fusion Development Matters 

Fusion development is gaining momentum because it’s a practical, adaptable approach to modern application development. It maximizes the talents of developers and non-coders alike, respecting their skill levels and preferences. This collaboration model accelerates innovation, enhances organizational agility, and reduces the pressure on IT teams — all critical in today’s fast-paced business environments. 

 

Key Benefits of Fusion Development Teams 

  • Accelerated Development: Key users can handle simpler tasks, shortening development cycles and enabling faster go-to-market timelines. Tool bridges allow seamless transition between development environments, being held together by a joint lobby for project creation, management, collaboration, monitoring or deployment. 
  • Enhanced Collaboration: Breaking down silos (e.g., between IT and business or backend and frontend teams) fosters more agile, collaborative, and efficient development cycles.  
  • Scalable Innovation: Prototyping can be extended to business experts, and automation can be initiated by those who will use it daily. Strong governance guidelines will be needed to ensure innovation never comes at the expense of compliance. 

 

Takeaway 

Fusion development bridges the gap between business agility and IT governance, offering a collaborative, flexible approach to application development. It also enables via seamless and context-sensitive tool bridges to maximize developer preferences and flexibility. It’s a cornerstone of modern digital transformation, evolving to make application development more accessible, scalable, and innovative for all.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model | Eighth Customer Roundtable
entry.content:
It's a long time since we had the last SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Customer Roundtable. And a lot of things happened since the last time. In June 2024 we released new major versions, version 8 for CAP Node.js stack and version 3 for the Java stack. Altogether we published more than 30 (!) Open Source components around the eco-system of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Information on cap-js-contributions you can find here and here, information on cap-java contributions are also available. 

In June, we had a phantastic re>≡CAP 2024 conference with hundreds of active visitors and participants, onsite and dialed in remotely. We touched topics like full stack BTP applications, CAP + Type Script, Performance, Embedded Analytics, Native OData Adapter, and even the application of GenAI technologies. Thus, the success story of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model continues.

But, more important than technology innovations are the users and customers of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. The more it is used, the more it is adopted in productive applications the more mature it becomes and the more it can ensured that technology and features met real life needs and the more we can increase quality and reliability. 

Thus the eighth SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is dedicated to your CAP experiences, requiremens and - also - your success stories of productive appliciations of the CAP framework. 

Consequently the agenda this time looks like the following:

  1. Intro with some reCAP Recordings and an outlook on Code Connect 2025
  2. Update from new CAP features and Capire documentation
  3. Customer Success Stories
  4. Q&A

 

Meeting Information


When: Thursday, December 12th, 2024, 11 AM EST | 5 PM CET

Where: Teams Meeting

Meeting Request Download: Link   

Who:  

    • All interested partners and stakeholders are invited to join: developers, enterprise architects, technical consultants…
    • The SAP Cloud Application Programming Model team: among others @TheSebastian  & me (as hosts), @Daniel7  (Chief Product Owner), @lilienthal (Head), and further team members, e.g. Product Owners and Architects
    • Anyone interested in learning about the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and exchange your ideas and feedback with other users and the product team


Looking forward to seeing you there!
Check also out the new Events page in our documentation and our Community page for updates on upcoming events.

entry.title: REVIEW: New Layout / Features for SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps now has a new design-time UI that is intended to make it easier to navigate around the tool. Like with anything new, it might take a little while to get used to it. Here's a review and a few tips.

Most of the change is a rearrangement of where to find things:

  • Putting things you use most often on the main page (e.g., Preview).
  • Hiding things you use less often (e.g., Auth).
  • Rearranging or renaming buttons to make them more sensible (e.g., Variable and Integrations).  

In addition, there are some new or recent capabilities that are worth getting to know, now that they are more prominent.  

 

New UI

Here is a quick schematic of how the menu items were rearranged.

The top main menu bar now organizes the main capabilities in a more logical way, based on the classic MVC (model-view-controller) paradigm:

  • User Interface: The "view"
  • Variables: Essentially, this is the "controller" or "simple logic" since variables are used to create bindings, that create a way to respond to data and user actions. For more advanced logic, the logic canvas remains in the same place. Instead of a toggle between View and Variables – which was always a bit awkward – you now have clear buttons to go from User Interface to Variables. 
  • Integrations: The "model" or data, which now can come not just from data services but from processes and actions. 
  • App Settings: Other app-wide tasks that are done infrequently or even just once. 

 

Preview

The preview and build capabilities – especially the preview – are things I want all the time, and now they have dedicated buttons on the UI canvas.

One of things I never liked is how many clicks it took to open a preview.

  • With the new versioning feature, it could take up to 5 clicks to open a preview:
    1. Launch
    2. Open Preview Portal
    3. Open Web Preview
    4. Settings (on your app)
    5. Open Preview (of specific version) 
  • The new exposed button saves you the first click.

What would be nice is to have a button on the UI canvas that opens the web preview for the editable version right away. 

And perhaps you can help me, but I never understood being in a specific project, saying preview ... 

... and then again having to specify which project I want to run. 

TIP: Whenever I see someone go to Launch tab each time they want to preview an app it makes my blood boil 😡. Open a preview window, and then whenever you save your changes to the project, the preview window will automatically update.

 

Build and Publish (deploy)

Similarly, to get to the build area you no longer have to deal with the preview option – the Publish button now takes you straight to where you build and deploy your app.

PET PEEVE: I don't like having an in-between dialog before getting directly to the build Area – they saved me a step by not mixing preview and publish, but now they lost me a step by having to choose between versioning and publish. 

Note that for each build you must specify either a version (more on those later) or the latest editable state of the app.

 

Versioning

This is how versioning works:

  • Whenever you click Save, a "state" of the app is preserved. You can always restore that state – restoring means that the state is copied back on top of the current editable state (the original state remains in the history in the same place).
  • To view the states, you click the History (clock) icon.
  • When you want to mark a state as a new version, perhaps so you can deploy it, you click Publish and then Create a Release Version and it creates a new version for you. Basically, it's a marker for a specific state.

Here's what you see when you click the History icon.

And remember, when you make a build, you cannot choose a state but rather a version (or the current editable state).

 

Pages

The new interface for working with pages lets you navigate to a page or create a new page without first opening the pages area.

Directly from the UI canvas, you get a dropdown with an option Add New Page. You can also navigate to Show All Pages, which takes you to the same pages area UI as we previously had. Note that the pages area (as of time of writing this blog) shows your pages in alphabetical order.

The dropdown also shows the current page plus up to 4 other pages that you can directly navigate to. After a little testing, my understanding is that the dropdown shows:

  • The current page, plus the 2 previous pages and the 2 next pages.
  • If you are on the 1st or 2nd page or the last or second to last page, the list will be adjusted to show you up to 5 pages around the current page. 

Important to note that when I click Show All Pages, the pages are shown in alphabetical order. In the dropdown, the pages are shown in the order you created them.

 

Default App Layout

By default, a new project will be shown laid out as a full-screen web project. In the past, the default layout was for a mobile phone.

However, if you choose a mobile layout, SAP Build Apps remembers that choice in all your projects in that tenant, and will set the layout to mobile the next time you create a new project.

I am told that although the original tool was designed primary for building cross-platform mobile apps, most partners and customers are using SAP Build Apps as a way to quickly create web apps without coding or using profession front-end tools.

Notice that the selector for choosing the "floorplan" for your app has moved to the right side, but it is basically the same: a zoom dropdown and a device dropdown. Notice that the choices for device have been simplified.  

 

What do you think?

Please comment:

  • What do you think of the changes?
  • What other changes would you want?

😺

entry.title: How SAP BTP Enhances Fiori Applications for Modern Workflows
entry.content:
Integrating SAP BTP Services into Fiori Applications

In this short article we’re looking at some core services provided by SAP BTP that can make our Fiori apps way better. BTP has tools that help us do things faster and make work easier. These are some of the services which my team have worked quite a lot lately and the examples shared below are just a high level explanation

With BTP, we can automate tasks (like creation of sales orders), make apps work well on mobile devices, set up notifications from other systems, and even add AI to help with predictions and smart decisions. All these services together make Fiori apps more flexible and powerful, so they actually fit what users need and save everyone a lot of hassle.

 

 

Core BTP Services for Fiori

SAP Build Process Automation (Workflow + IRPA)

SAP BTP’s Workflow service automates and manages business processes seamlessly within Fiori applications. Workflow allows for the creation of multi-step approval chains, task assignments, and process tracking. For example, an organization can implement a purchase order approval workflow within a Fiori app, where each approval or rejection step is automatically updated in real time. This eliminates manual processing and reduces turnaround time, enhancing operational efficiency.

IRPA complements workflows by automating repetitive, manual tasks like data entry or validations. For instance, an IRPA bot can process incoming invoices, validate them against purchase orders, and flag discrepancies for approval in the Fiori app.

Process Visibility offers end-to-end insights into workflows, providing transparency and analytics. Stakeholders can monitor live status, identify bottlenecks, and ensure SLAs are met, enabling proactive process management.

Business Rules provide flexibility in defining approval criteria. For example, an organization can configure dynamic rules based on factors like transaction value or department, ensuring that high-value approvals escalate automatically to senior management. This customization ensures streamlined and compliant operations.

Practical Scenario for SBPA: Using SAP SBPA for automating tenant onboarding in a real estate app.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Tenant applies to move into a property.
  • Step 2: Workflow triggers and sends an approval request to the property manager.
  • Step 3: Property manager reviews and clicks “Approve” or “Reject” in the app.
  • Step 4: If approved, a notification goes to finance for deposit processing.
  • Step 5: Finance team processes payment and updates status.
  • Step 6: Maintenance team gets a notification to prep the apartment (cleaning, repairs, etc.).
  • Step 7: Once everything’s ready, tenant gets a welcome message with move-in date.

Benefits:

  • Saves time with automated approvals.
  • Clear process, everyone knows what’s next.
  • Reduces delays; no need to email or follow up manually.
           

Mobile Services

SAP Mobile Platform for BTP enable Fiori applications to be fully accessible on mobile devices, with features supporting multi-device compatibility and offline functionality. Users can interact with applications even without an active internet connection, with updates automatically synchronized once connectivity is restored. For example, in field operations, technicians can update service records on a Fiori app even in remote areas, ensuring that data is captured in real time and reflected across the organization when they’re back online.

 

Practical Scenario for Mobile Services: Maintenance team using a Fiori app to log repairs on-site.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Worker opens the Fiori app on their mobile device while at the repair site.
  • Step 2: They fill out details (e.g., parts replaced, issue resolved) in the app, even without internet.
  • Step 3: App saves the data offline.
  • Step 4: Later, once back online, the app syncs the data with central records.

Benefits:

  • Workers can log repairs immediately, no waiting for internet.
  • Data syncs when there’s a connection, so no missed updates.
  • Reduces paperwork, everything’s in the app.

 

Advanced Event Mesh

SAP Event Mesh enables asynchronous communication, ensuring that Fiori applications can respond to events from various backend systems without delay. This service facilitates real-time event-driven responses, useful in scenarios like order processing or inventory updates. For example, when stock levels reach a predefined threshold, Event Mesh triggers a notification in the Fiori app, allowing users to act immediately to restock, thus preventing delay.

Practical Scenario for Event Mesh: Using Event Mesh in a Fiori app for tracking supply chain inventory.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: Warehouse hits low inventory level for an important product.
  • Step 2: Event Mesh detects this and triggers an alert.
  • Step 3: Fiori app instantly shows a notification for the purchasing team.
  • Step 4: Purchasing team checks the notification and orders more stock.

Benefits:

  • Teams can act immediately to prevent shortages.
  • No need to check inventory manually; they get alerts.
  • Keeps operations smooth by handling restocking on time.

SAP AI Core and AI Foundation

SAP AI Core provide machine learning and AI capabilities that integrate directly into Fiori applications. These services support predictive analytics and automated decision-making, helping users make data-driven decisions efficiently. A practical use case is in a sales application, where predictive analytics might forecast inventory requirements based on historical data, allowing users to prepare and manage resources proactively

 

Practical Scenario for SAP AI Core: Sales team using Fiori app with AI to manage inventory.

Process Steps:

  • Step 1: AI model in Fiori app analyses past sales data and predicts future demand.
  • Step 2: Based on prediction, the app suggests optimal stock levels.
  • Step 3: Sales team sees recommended order amounts right in the app.
  • Step 4: They order the suggested amount to avoid running out or over-ordering.

Benefits:

  • Helps sales team avoid stock issues by planning ahead.
  • Reduces wasted stock, as predictions are data-driven.
  • Makes ordering decisions faster and more accurate.

Conclusion

Using BTP services really levels up our Fiori apps. We can set up automated workflows, support mobile use, get instant alerts when things change, and bring in AI to plan smarter. These features make the apps more helpful and let them handle new demands.

It makes the app experience smoother for everyone using it and helps the business run better too. With BTP, our Fiori apps aren’t just tools—they become something people can rely on every day to get things done more easily.

entry.title: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 1
entry.content:
Business Context

While working day in and day out on SAP BTP use cases, we have noticed a growing inclination among customers towards “Automation” specially with SAP BTP SAP Build Process Automation. This trend has led to the development of various side-by-side custom extensions on SAP BTP utilizing SAP Build Process Automation.

Problem Statement

One of the most common requests we receive from multiple customers is for a mechanism that allows email-based approval without the need to manually log into the My Inbox application.

Currently, there is no out-of-the-box capability available to fulfill this requirement. Therefore, we will be developing our own custom module to address this need.

In this three-part blog series, my colleague & BTP Architect @Adwait_Fadnavis  and I will provide insights on how to implement Email-based approval using SAP Build Process Automation capabilities.

We have prepared the content for this series in three blogs, where we will be diving deep into Design, Development, and Testing.

  • Blog 1 (This blog) – This blog will focus on the Business process, context, problem statement, end to end development flow, and required prerequisites.
  • Blog 2 This blog will focus on development and will provide a detailed walkthrough of the development process.
  • Blog 3: This blog will focus on complete end-to-end testing, helping to understand the overall objective.

Scenario Explanation

In this scenario, a workflow will be triggered using SAP Build Process Automation, which will send an email notification to the respective approver.

“The approver will receive the email notification and can reply to this email with either “Approve” or “Reject.” 

Based on the response, the corresponding workflow task will be approved or rejected.

Important Considerations

  1. Valid Approver Verification: The approver must be a valid approver, and the email must come from the approver’s registered email address. If the email does not come from the proper approver, or if the response is not “Approved” or “Reject,” the system must send an email back indicating that the response is not valid.
  2. Unique Request Number: Each approval request must generate a unique request number. This number will be used to validate the response against the original request, ensuring that the approval or rejection is correctly matched to the corresponding workflow task.

Disclaimer:  We can't recall the email after the approval/rejection.

End to End Business Process Flow: Streamlining Approval Processes with SAP BPA Email-Based Automation

End to End Process Flow : Email based Approval

  1. Workflow Initiation

The process begins when an SAP BPA workflow is triggered. This could be due to various business events, such as a purchase order requiring approval.
